Subject: Pitch Update: fotostax - Termsheet Received / 2M Seed / 4D Spatial AI
Body:
Hi Corner team,
Quick update: fotostax just received a termsheet for our 2M Seed round and we are now in late-stage talks with two other VCs.
Our deck is here: https://docsend.com/view/faxwbufd49y4fbwg [docsend.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docsend.com/view/faxwbufd49y4fbwg__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!gQojMV3XFC0hMV4PSAkav8zT9uS2xly-XelPxCUMTqIZ29z3NpusVrswuy8nafRDr9yRO6tnaDocSQ$> .
Briefly on fotostax: we are building semantic 4D data capture tech for world models and embodied AI. We have developed a hardware-software co-design to capture 4D datasets that are currently unavailable to frontier model labs, bridging the gap between physical habits and digital intelligence.
I saw Corner's focus on identifying iconic, category-defining companies. Given our current traction and the technical pedigree of our cap table (including the Fitbit Co-Founder), I believe we have built a foundational data engine for the spatial era. Please let me know if you would like to discuss further.

Best,
Jonathan


Jonathan McBride
Co-founder and CEO
fotostax [fotostax.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://fotostax.com__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!gQojMV3XFC0hMV4PSAkav8zT9uS2xly-XelPxCUMTqIZ29z3NpusVrswuy8nafRDr9yRO6u026OR0g$>

Where Investors Still Lean In 


Inside: five fresh rounds that sold a sharper use of funds. 

 <https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/o/u001.FuLJlKVdt_T_-Vwb3NBKiw/4pm/1wHohCxJQ5eNxeKSYVzrww/ho.gif> 

 <https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/f9fa19c8-222f-4da6-be99-ed7a46091dd8/Capital_Raise__2_.png?t=1773839280> 	
What stood out in the last 24 hours was not raw volume. It was specificity. Patlytics did not pitch “legal AI.” It pitched patent work. Modus did not pitch “finance transformation.” It pitched audit workflows that still run like it is a different decade. Golden went after the gap between raw data and usable answers. 

Aria focused on the networking layer choking AI clusters. Natter turned enterprise feedback from a slow research project into something teams can use in hours. Different categories. Same move. The best stories name the operational failure first, then make the next step feel fundable. 

If you are raising now, take the hint. The pitch gets stronger when you stop selling the whole category and start selling the exact problem that keeps showing up in the buyer’s day. Clear failure mode first. Clear unlock second. Everything else can come later. 

  	

The quiet way to stay on LinkedIn without actually being on LinkedIn.

 <https://beehiiv-images-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/asset/file/a145991e-fea2-4c4f-864a-e739de6126cb/image_-_2026-04-09T005531.429.png?t=1775667345> 	

I know someone who has been showing up on LinkedIn consistently for months — expert-level posts, active profile, steady engagement. They haven't written a single post manually. They use Postgetti. It writes the posts, schedules everything, and handles comment replies. The kind of tool that makes people ask how you do it. They're offering 10% off with code NEWSLETTER if you want to try it.

 <https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8bb6906f-6935-4781-a2de-4772de961ef8/Capital_Raise_Elements.png?t=1774271729> 	
 <https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e161157b-70fe-458f-84c9-d456c739129c/Capital_Raise_-_Email_Photo.png?t=1775664201> 	

Patlytics raised $40M by staying painfully narrow

Patlytics closed a $40 million Series B led by SignalFire. The company is built for patent workflows across drafting, infringement analysis, and portfolio management, and it says it already serves more than 40% of Am Law 100 IP practices. That is why the round works. It is not broad legal tech. It is one expensive corner of legal work that rewards precision. See full article. What this means for your raise: The more specialized the pain, the easier it is to justify urgency. Founders do not need a giant market story if the workflow is high-value and deeply annoying. 

 <https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/637fe8d4-aac3-438f-86d6-670131451d83/Capital_Raise_-_Email_Photo__1_.png?t=1775664397> 	

Modus raised $85M by making the audit feel overdue for change

Modus announced $85 million in Seed and Series A funding, led by Lightspeed Venture Partners. Its pitch is simple and sharp: audits remain a trust-critical process, but the tooling and workflow have barely moved, so the company is pairing AI-native software with investments in audit-first accounting firms to modernize the work without forcing those firms to blow themselves up. See full article. [link.mail.beehiiv.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.sCzFM8RRujTr36BpbxXKICx7Oy1N2uyu958vi63U4Q8_FwEKwKB2NBh_BaS6c6R-HdVG-3a7URlcQSJytSxgULCBgy1XduxhoUEZ-5cg3pQB_B7dvXD4mU0M7zVefrPjQvvqaaSnEe8YPQ8JJ_DvhY3PfqH9Ai8HB-QCZh_4iz0UBHEX2qP_ykxN1NMXMP-XxoCcPYtQTE_5Zdkaw2rSnbkmebkHSsl5YOlddUXus4HvHO40CT23mSn_RLkSpLTBUy6DjDssC-0ka3NUmafIsuc2HUI7cZ_CkMN6Msf5nbqxq-B86-805wB9TPbls33r7hoKZeI7NedQMb0vY6Mwi57VXK320H1QElR9SAWz8u0/4pm/1wHohCxJQ5eNxeKSYVzrww/h3/h001.g8kRy9fG6RDWMy7ntWMHAX-aWLdR5u_TUgvs8LeE6eg__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!g9lsE5hodR4Fx_FhhbTYjGCjwZPmg0V6ewYbbCfJO9ki8aKSVBS60eVAp--zFtVgkasvxnd80qm2R1KRCs_eHXyZ0fSCSpQCF-w$> 

What this means for your raise: A strong story often comes from showing why an old, durable workflow has reached its breaking point. If the current process still matters but no longer scales, that is a compelling “why now.” 

 <https://hostingjournalist.com/uploads/news-pictures/2057-zutphen-blog-post-image-20260408045359.webp> 	

Golden Analytics raised $7M by collapsing the trip from data to answer

Golden Analytics launched from stealth with $7 million in seed funding from NEA and Madrona, with participation from Breakers. The company says users can go from raw datasets to shareable dashboards in as few as two clicks, which is a much cleaner framing than generic BI language. It is really selling less friction between the question and the decision. See full article. [link.mail.beehiiv.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.5dpAuThbA_vMaB_J8xCGI4vp3Z9_CjWlX95OJ-PtYTEDPIKlksfXmU1tML6MRdaeWszJ-xSXQz_FSq51milgnUdXscW-kOgnwBHH4fx0_kznZRm6p6jAygGq_z41rJHCY8IHS-FIkWjisqsDuIHWGf9U3esre_j2ZfsgtWVyQ1gIDBRYvLL1I-fLmw3cgcSN7GSA-xnNGF_ivZAt5A8chpUs2omsNcKN2kDG0ysdifAUPc6CokWAAOzdVa9WBRd16Xf18W7yQVcElHXkkCv3b3m6kYYgLN4zQxZ32C6UD5SYN2yf6IyyU5l6Q3CIeCArrPitrMILLmfgjesSNa2SD0ONjaa14tKsHeQxBCUlnN2duZj3rWSJ7rq8LaYzQE6ANofqeU9lDN7PH118KXUUig/4pm/1wHohCxJQ5eNxeKSYVzrww/h4/h001.zGGMqdzRxIw8sVDcZEwCSGV5gOICkQ2bAhDNIYNvAHI__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!g9lsE5hodR4Fx_FhhbTYjGCjwZPmg0V6ewYbbCfJO9ki8aKSVBS60eVAp--zFtVgkasvxnd80qm2R1KRCs_eHXyZ0fSC4ed06ew$> 

What this means for your raise: When your product sits in a crowded category, anchor the story around a simpler before-and-after. Buyers do not purchase “better analytics.” They experience less delay between messy inputs and useful decisions. 

 <https://www.startupresearcher.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Frcid5p3a%2Fproduction%2F48aaf8f63533f6373b18c9eda8f21ee7fb742678-1200x630.png%3Ffm%3Dwebp%26w%3D1440%26h%3D720&w=1920&q=75> 	

Aria raised $125M because AI still needs better pipes

Aria Networks secured $125 million in its first funding round from Sutter Hill Ventures, Atreides Management, Valor Equity Partners, and Eclipse Ventures. The company is building networking infrastructure for AI data centers and says it already has customer orders and production deployments. That matters because the pitch is not about faster chips. It is about the layer that moves the data that those chips depend on. See full article. [link.mail.beehiiv.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.5dpAuThbA_vMaB_J8xCGI4vp3Z9_CjWlX95OJ-PtYTHr_oYMsg-SP3xT09Lo6houbB3Y4f-fMzSCvyoe-4qBidxDYsofQNDE6p1LMXgnqX5ryQMd22X887ZElfGR0ZHxOkNVZS_ksaKpllflSDQf3kFGr7umXbZLAIIPHX-mFD6_XyL2cUSNFcbpuT2AvGCv1S_Rnr4uBLOL-aj9eotOuUT4zwLtLhCRfLXgNJhn3dhV4HcBhhTLjyN9ariqfw1Kf0FJtyaAGUhyzWRYoSmUCNgHw-9V4CdiBlKH85Skc3N3ljjAR54d0-wDtWgAeRmnYHDHyLAiTzGbvSFVYqGmcwFz9pwkcLPFj5eBkK75WoputaWFq4LcLM9V3dAsmPhF/4pm/1wHohCxJQ5eNxeKSYVzrww/h5/h001.GVbdxQE0Ge7b5RhCzwcyotiWzFJRr3CjEsUBLqlzTyM__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!g9lsE5hodR4Fx_FhhbTYjGCjwZPmg0V6ewYbbCfJO9ki8aKSVBS60eVAp--zFtVgkasvxnd80qm2R1KRCs_eHXyZ0fSCNJLkjo0$> 

What this means for your raise: Infrastructure companies win when they explain the missing layer, not just the big trend above it. If your product keeps the rest of the stack from stalling, say that plainly. 

 <https://images.axios.com/WOy498VTN6zBgow3ObyNjldJnZ4=/2022/12/09/192736-1670614056719.jpg> 	

Natter raised $23M by making enterprise listening feel usable again

Natter raised $23 million in a round led by Renegade Partners, with participation from Kindred Capital, Costanoa Ventures, Rackhouse Ventures, Village Global, and Asymmetric Capital Partners. Its platform runs thousands of one-on-one video conversations with employees, customers, and stakeholders, then turns them into usable insights for sales, HR, strategy, and policy teams in hours instead of weeks. See full article. [link.mail.beehiiv.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.5dpAuThbA_vMaB_J8xCGI4vp3Z9_CjWlX95OJ-PtYTH7AZaNm_IDWjT0cM39mGsJKKZV1Ts0Ma5e8r2qWJGdvq76sr7yqk5oSsfl5qMugCsz2rsOEae5OnGSDewGd8TshlKaMhrjpbItvxkHZ9SSXAUPhYIlPO9jdR18GgvY8_xy3Ix2tCj-rSabO0nMOHQ5qbVR_56yWl9n_pQPab-FBypudEwNb1mzKZYD5oAatq2UkT3OS1LvJstteVSL2gYuoEgINNL3hj7IybR4TgDVNyb6pdI6JYc6QElpxVkx85NYRNWHqYlV2k2ZANKNhFNoRCsrf05UWJ9CM7Ju-gGC5g/4pm/1wHohCxJQ5eNxeKSYVzrww/h6/h001.l8D0JLS1f_a_esZQPDyeHibGLCxaWJ4kJ6vlII30ie8__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!g9lsE5hodR4Fx_FhhbTYjGCjwZPmg0V6ewYbbCfJO9ki8aKSVBS60eVAp--zFtVgkasvxnd80qm2R1KRCs_eHXyZ0fSCGK7ySto$> 

What this means for your raise: Research-heavy workflows can still be a good wedge if you reframe them around speed and decision quality. The stronger pitch is not “better insight.” It is “less waiting for insight.”

Most founders still explain the company before they explain the round. That is backward. 

Use this:

We have already shown that [specific thing] works.

This round gets us to [specific milestone] by [date].

If we hit it, the business changes in three ways: [growth], [proof], and [leverage].

Example:

We have already shown that buyers adopt this product when it removes a painful workflow inside an existing team.

This round gets us to deployment across larger accounts by Q1 next year.

If we hit it, deal size improves, the buyer case gets stronger, and the next round becomes easier to price.

That framing works because it gives the investor a straight line from capital to consequence. You are not asking them to fund “more time.” You are asking them to fund one visible step forward.

Startup Spotlight: Argebordro

Each week, we highlight one startup from the SeedScope ecosystem.

Argebordro, a SaaS Payroll & HR Platform for Turkish R&D Companies
Valuation: $4.8M | Sector: SaaS / HR Tech | Stage: Pre-Seed

This startup has a 1.7x ROI Multiplier.
Argebordro.com.tr automates the full payroll and HR workflow for Turkish R&D firms  including SGK incentive separation, PDKS-driven R&D day tracking, Technopark portal submissions, travel expenses, and digital leave management. Built specifically for the compliance complexity of Turkey's R&D incentive landscape, the platform delivers direct monetary value by eliminating costly calculation errors and manual reporting overhead.
Key Highlights

Role-based SaaS platform with full Technopark and PDKS integrations, purpose-built for Turkish R&D compliance requirements
Automates SGK and tax incentive calculations, approval workflows, and government portal submissions
Go-to-market via direct sales and free demos, with an established partnership with Kapsam Denetim
Operates in a high-growth HR and payroll SaaS market with steady digitalization and increasing demand for localized R&D solutions
Highly differentiated from broader competitors like Kolay IK, Logo Yazılım, and SAP SuccessFactors through niche focus and compliance depth

SeedScope Assessment
Argebordro.com.tr scores 76, reflecting solid fundamentals and clear market fit within Turkey's compliance-driven R&D payroll segment. With a TRL of 9, a strong sales score of 9, and an experienced team score of 5, the platform demonstrates production-ready technology and effective commercial execution. Key strengths include excellent scalability, strong market differentiation, and a solution that delivers quantifiable value to accountants, HR, and finance teams inside R&D firms. Primary risks center on low founder dedication  with part-time involvement potentially limiting growth velocity alongside limited disclosed traction, pricing transparency, and brand recognition at this early stage. For investors seeking niche SaaS exposure in an underserved compliance market, Argebordro.com.tr presents a compelling pre-seed opportunity with manageable downside.

Startup Spotlight: Argebordro

Each week, we highlight one startup from the SeedScope ecosystem.

Argebordro, a SaaS Payroll & HR Platform for Turkish R&D Companies
Valuation: $4.8M | Sector: SaaS / HR Tech | Stage: Pre-Seed

This startup has a 1.7x ROI Multiplier.
Argebordro.com.tr automates the full payroll and HR workflow for Turkish R&D firms  including SGK incentive separation, PDKS-driven R&D day tracking, Technopark portal submissions, travel expenses, and digital leave management. Built specifically for the compliance complexity of Turkey's R&D incentive landscape, the platform delivers direct monetary value by eliminating costly calculation errors and manual reporting overhead.
Key Highlights

Role-based SaaS platform with full Technopark and PDKS integrations, purpose-built for Turkish R&D compliance requirements
Automates SGK and tax incentive calculations, approval workflows, and government portal submissions
Go-to-market via direct sales and free demos, with an established partnership with Kapsam Denetim
Operates in a high-growth HR and payroll SaaS market with steady digitalization and increasing demand for localized R&D solutions
Highly differentiated from broader competitors like Kolay IK, Logo Yazılım, and SAP SuccessFactors through niche focus and compliance depth

SeedScope Assessment
Argebordro.com.tr scores 76, reflecting solid fundamentals and clear market fit within Turkey's compliance-driven R&D payroll segment. With a TRL of 9, a strong sales score of 9, and an experienced team score of 5, the platform demonstrates production-ready technology and effective commercial execution. Key strengths include excellent scalability, strong market differentiation, and a solution that delivers quantifiable value to accountants, HR, and finance teams inside R&D firms. Primary risks center on low founder dedication  with part-time involvement potentially limiting growth velocity alongside limited disclosed traction, pricing transparency, and brand recognition at this early stage. For investors seeking niche SaaS exposure in an underserved compliance market, Argebordro.com.tr presents a compelling pre-seed opportunity with manageable downside.

Subject: AlgoArena - Transforming CS Education and Assessment (Pre-Seed)
Body:
Hi Andrew,

I'm reaching out because of your work at Corner Ventures and your interest in innovative technology startups.

I'm Eli, co-founder of AlgoArena (algoarena.net), a platform transforming how people learn, compete, build, and prove CS skills. My co-founder Andy Luu and I are both students at Swarthmore College. It started at dinner after a basketball game. I showed Andy a list of ideas I keep on my phone, and he pointed at 'LeetCode 1v1' and said 'Hey, that seems like it would be fun to make!' Two months later we'd curated 5,000+ problems, built real-time matchmaking and ELO rankings, piloted a Classroom mode with a CS class at Bryn Mawr, and expanded the vision into a full platform for learning, competing, building, and proving CS skills, from the classroom to the career.

Here's what we've built and where we're headed.

Compete (Live) - Real-time 1v1 coding battles with ELO-based matchmaking across 5,000+ curated problems and 16 languages. We've had 1,000+ matches completed and 500+ users so far. LeetCode made coding practice educational. We made it a sport.

Learn (Live) - Classroom mode is Kahoot fundamentally tailored for CS. Professors run live sessions with multiple choice, true/false, interactive puzzles, and full coding problems. We already piloted it with a competitive programming class at Bryn Mawr College, where students said things like "It was fire" and "I gained confidence in my ability to actually solve a problem." We're also building gamified AI courses that teach computational thinking through interactive puzzles (no syntax required) and an agentic builder that lets anyone create real applications and export to GitHub.

Prove (Coming Soon) - AI-native assessments that actually test how developers work today. Recruiters can toggle between manual coding and AI-assisted development. Unlike HackerRank or Codility, we provide a real development environment, not a dumbed-down chatbot, capturing prompt engineering ability, multitasking, attention to detail, and interaction patterns. The disconnect between how developers actually work and how they're tested is massive and getting worse with AI.

Why now?
LeetCode ($300M+ ARR) proved demand for coding practice but never innovated on engagement. Kahoot proved gamified learning works but never touched CS. HackerRank tests candidates the old way while real development has fundamentally changed with AI. We sit at the intersection of all three, and we're expanding beyond them with AI-native courses and building tools that make tech creation accessible to everyone.

We're raising a pre-seed round. Would you be open to a 15-minute call?

Best,
Eli Young
Co-founder, AlgoArena
Swarthmore College '26
algoarena.net | eyoung4@swarthmore.edu

Reply "Unsubscribe" to be removed from future outreach.

Subject: Moltmarket - AI-native prediction markets for India's 100M+ crypto users. Pre-seed open.
Body:
Hi Name,

Andrew Hall's piece on prediction markets going 'bigger, broader, and smarter' reads like a product spec — so I built it.

Moltmarket is a decentralized prediction market where AI agents are autonomous market makers (with wallets, stakes, and performance scores), LLM oracles resolve disputes trustlessly on-chain, and social discourse auto-spawns new markets in real time.

It directly addresses the two scaling problems your thesis identified: contract volume and resolution at scale.

We're live on Polygon Amoy testnet today → https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://moltmarketai.vercel.app/__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!h2h3VnNet6d6N--gqH59j4PeLRir7Vx_f-FzxuDm_2_jWHPwPiCGQETy0OYbuGGox0TKh9JAWH0hp2rICdgT6wYQZPsj-Q$

What we've built:
• AI agents provide 24/7 liquidity autonomously — no human market makers
• LLM oracle network resolves outcomes in minutes, trustlessly on-chain
• Social signals (Twitter/X, Reddit) auto-spawn markets in real time

Traction on Polygon Amoy testnet:
• 3,000+ markets · 1,200 wallets · $150K simulated liquidity

Looking for a pre-seed conversation with someone that understands both ai and prediction market scale. Would a 15–20 minute call work?

Best,
Vansh Sharma
Founder, Moltmarket
vansh.sharma.cse@gmail.com
+91 9773553703

Subject: Moltmarket - AI-native prediction markets for India's 100M+ crypto users. Pre-seed open.
Body:
Hi Name,

Andrew Hall's piece on prediction markets going 'bigger, broader, and smarter' reads like a product spec — so I built it.

Moltmarket is a decentralized prediction market where AI agents are autonomous market makers (with wallets, stakes, and performance scores), LLM oracles resolve disputes trustlessly on-chain, and social discourse auto-spawns new markets in real time.

It directly addresses the two scaling problems your thesis identified: contract volume and resolution at scale.

We're live on Polygon Amoy testnet today → https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://moltmarketai.vercel.app/__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!iY17OXT4Rp952VbqZHBdi--GZ5_APzrwIvaNalUqbZgLPLGsaPFQwnIowcgR9FUJpEJ2jJSgd0Sgaiwtw7rKGvMSuELFOg$

What we've built:
• AI agents provide 24/7 liquidity autonomously — no human market makers
• LLM oracle network resolves outcomes in minutes, trustlessly on-chain
• Social signals (Twitter/X, Reddit) auto-spawn markets in real time

Traction on Polygon Amoy testnet:
• 3,000+ markets · 1,200 wallets · $150K simulated liquidity

Looking for a pre-seed conversation with someone that understands both ai and prediction market scale. Would a 15–20 minute call work?

Best,
Vansh Sharma
Founder, Moltmarket
vansh.sharma.cse@gmail.com
+91 9773553703
Pitch Deck: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1UfkrJRYPVxCucTRGDphyy--H6PnshQuK/edit?usp=drive_link&ouid=101642786713983395628&rtpof=true&sd=true__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!iY17OXT4Rp952VbqZHBdi--GZ5_APzrwIvaNalUqbZgLPLGsaPFQwnIowcgR9FUJpEJ2jJSgd0Sgaiwtw7rKGvM_5cweag$

Subject: Lighthouse Avionics — Bridge Round
Body:
Hi Andrew,

 

Lighthouse reached out to your firm in late 2023. I’m Tyler, CEO of Lighthouse Avionics, and I wanted to personally reintroduce Lighthouse to you!

 

Just like a lighthouse guides ships safely to shore, Lighthouse Avionics illuminates the airspace and guides aircraft safely through it. We build ground-based sensor systems that watch the sky so drones can fly beyond line of sight, safely and legally.

 

We had been planning a $2M seed round, but a strategic acquisition opportunity landed in front of us with a narrow window to act. We’re raising $750K as a bridge to fund it. Deck attached.

 

Even if the timing isn’t perfect on your end, we’d still love to connect. Would you have a few minutes for a quick call?

 

Regards,

Tyler Bair | CEO & Co-Founder

tyler@lighthouseavionics.com <mailto:tyler@lighthouseavionics.com>  | 614-551-8142

Subject: TokuGo — Pre-Seed Round (Up to £1M) | Spatial Commerce
Body:
Hello,

I’m reaching out because I’m building TokuGo — the Spatial Commerce Layer, and I’d genuinely value your feedback.

TokuGo turns cities into interactive discovery environments where people unlock nearby places, offers and rewards as they move through the real world using AI and AR.

The platform is built for mobile today and designed for smart glasses tomorrow.

Before raising capital we’ve already built the core infrastructure:

• 400+ retail locations mapped
• 800+ deals deployed through our CMS
• working MVP + prototype
• London pilot ready

We’ve just opened a £750k SAFE pre-seed round (20% discount, ~£15M cap) to launch the London pilot and onboard founding brand partners.

If you’re open to it, I’d really appreciate 15–20 minutes of feedback — I’d also be happy to walk you through a quick demo.

In our first year of operations, we generated just under $1M in net revenue and currently service 60+ clients across the platform. As the business scales, we are now on track to exceed last year’s revenue in our second quarter.

We’ve started conversations with a small group of strategic investors, and I’d love to briefly share more about what we’re building. I have also attached our deck to this email.

Would you be open to a 15–20 minute call sometime this week?

	Dear Andrew,

	 

	I understand your fund is currently in market and raising capital, and I wanted to introduce Claridge Capital Partners.

	 

	Claridge is a single-family office investing across alternative assets with a focus on venture capital, growth equity, private equity, digital assets, and select strategic opportunities. We view ourselves as long-term, value-added partners to managers and entrepreneurs building durable, category-defining businesses.

	 

	Our platform spans seed through growth via primary commitments, direct investments, co-investments, and secondaries. We are highly active allocators and take a lifecycle approach to manager partnerships — supporting GPs not only with capital, but also with strategic introductions, co-investment participation, and access to aligned institutional and family office LPs within our network.

	 

	In addition to investing our own capital, we selectively introduce and syndicate with value-added LP partners when strategically appropriate. We frequently co-invest alongside managers in opportunities where we have domain conviction.

	 

	Recent activity includes:

	 

	• Participation in a ~$100B restructuring of one of the largest U.S. satellite operators

	• Seeding a private equity fund focused on satellite and defense technologies

	• Investment in a top-tier global cryptocurrency exchange

	• Participation in multiple SPAC transactions

	• Commitment to a leading technology-focused venture platform

	• Investment in a premier life sciences-focused fund

	 

	As we evaluate new fund opportunities, I would welcome the opportunity to schedule time to learn more about your strategy and discuss potential alignment.

	 

	Best regards,

Subject: Introductory call
Body:
Dear Andrew,

 

I understand your fund is currently in market and raising capital, and I wanted to introduce Claridge Capital Partners.

 

Claridge is a single-family office investing across alternative assets with a focus on venture capital, growth equity, private equity, digital assets, and select strategic opportunities. We view ourselves as long-term, value-added partners to managers and entrepreneurs building durable, category-defining businesses.

 

Our platform spans seed through growth via primary commitments, direct investments, co-investments, and secondaries. We are highly active allocators and take a lifecycle approach to manager partnerships — supporting GPs not only with capital, but also with strategic introductions, co-investment participation, and access to aligned institutional and family office LPs within our network.

 

In addition to investing our own capital, we selectively introduce and syndicate with value-added LP partners when strategically appropriate. We frequently co-invest alongside managers in opportunities where we have domain conviction.

 

Recent activity includes:

 

• Participation in a ~$100B restructuring of one of the largest U.S. satellite operators

• Seeding a private equity fund focused on satellite and defense technologies

• Investment in a top-tier global cryptocurrency exchange

• Participation in multiple SPAC transactions

• Commitment to a leading technology-focused venture platform

• Investment in a premier life sciences-focused fund

 

As we evaluate new fund opportunities, I would welcome the opportunity to schedule time to learn more about your strategy and discuss potential alignment.

 

Best regards,

Neil Vereshchagin
Partner
Claridge Capital Partners
T. +44 7947 814 817

Subject: The control layer enterprise AI is missing - Xybern
Body:
Enterprise AI is shifting from tools people use to agents that act autonomously, updating records, triggering workflows, calling APIs without human review.


Most enterprises have no gatekeeper between those agents and their production systems. Xybern is that gatekeeper.


We sit in the runtime execution path of enterprise AI systems, intercepting reasoning steps and tool calls before they execute. Not a dashboard. Not a monitoring layer. A control plane, the layer all enterprise AI actions pass through before anything happens.


Why this matters now:

• AI agents and LLMs executing autonomously make fragmented per-product guardrails insufficient, enterprises need cross-system enforcement

• Vendors can make their own systems safe. They cannot govern AI behaviour across your entire organisation

• Every internal copilot, agent, and workflow a company builds needs to route through a control layer. That layer is Xybern


Why we win:

• We sit in the inference path, not alongside it, enforcement, not observation

• Active pilot in legal services, financial services pipeline across two continents

• Founded by a PhD AI researcher with 10+ years production deployment experience and a co-founder who scaled enterprise deals at Salesforce

• Backed by NVIDIA Inception and AWS for Startups


We're raising $1M Pre-Seed to convert pilots to paid contracts and build the GTM motion.


Would you be open to a 15-minute call? I have attached a one-pager. Happy to walk you through. 


Dr Charalambos Theodorou

Founder & CEO | Xybern.com

NVIDIA Inception | AWS for Startups

________________________________

If you'd rather not hear from me, reply with "unsubscribe" and I'll remove you immediately.

Subject: AI Coach clones for athletes?
Body:
I’m reaching out because your investment focus overlaps with what we’re building at MyVisionLabs.

We’re developing real-time computer vision infrastructure for sports academies and coaches. Our system ingests live camera streams, runs fine-tuned vision models to analyze biomechanics, and feeds outputs into an agentic orchestration layer for live AI-based coaching. Over time, we build a structured player graph for each athlete and coach, enabling us to make hyper-personalized virtual coach/ player assistants. While we are focused on sports and fitness, the core technology has broad applications across different sectors.

We’re part of NVIDIA Inception, received a $50K AWS grant, and are already deploying with academies in California. We’re raising a $1.2–1.5M pre-seed to scale academy licensing. 

Would be glad to walk through the architecture and traction.

Regards,
Yogesh Agarwal

MyVisionLabs, Inc.

Subject: ﻿Andrew﻿, A Personal Note: Konzortia Semi-Annual Update & Limited Priority Opportunity
Body:
Dear ﻿Andrew﻿,
 

I hope you and your family are well and that 2026 has started strongly for you. I wanted to reach out personally with a concise update on Konzortia’s progress and to share a limited, priority investment opportunity before we finalize institutional terms.  

1) Momentum & Product — MVP Live, curated alpha access

We’ve moved from prototype to execution: the Alpha Hub MVP is live and currently being used in active validation by a curated group of allocators who joined our focus groups during development. Their real-world feedback has already accelerated product improvements and commercial positioning. Participation in MVP use is exclusive to that curated cohort — we’re opening a very limited number of additional access slots to priority investors before broader rollout. 

 

You can see the platform in action in our MVP Overview Video [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=8ad72c92-e9aa-4090-81e5-0851821de8c8&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fdocsend.com*2fview*2fyuwve58kivmcph7q&ValidityToken=7PfST*2fL6KJ*2fbvnimTNnAYyBXaYYwXJi8pOLo6quiN7c*3d__;JSUlJSUlJSU!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCbcmZ53w$>  here.
 

Read the full product and validation details in the Semi-Annual Report [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=fb568161-3336-44ff-8c58-8568b8498d62&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fdocsend.com*2fview*2fb5asv6pv5cy38hfs&ValidityToken=L7ZNjvJ7YoZpUB5d9Iq3E8OQF5J07JumkmII93h4iIE*3d__;JSUlJSUl!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCaldEfzM$>  here.

If you’re interested in becoming an investor in the company, schedule a meeting [%20https] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://*20https:/*calendly.com/walter-gomez-kc/priority-investor-zoom-meeting-with-the-found-clone-1.__;JS8!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCQNGdts0$>  with the Founder:

 

2) Commercial Traction & Partnerships — institutional runway forming 

We’ve formalized syndication and advisory relationships and advanced commercial conversations with institutional counterparties. This is not speculative — we’ve documented partnerships and term progress in the report and are preparing the corporate and cap-table improvements necessary for institutional participation.  

 

See partner and commercial update in the Semi-Annual Report [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=fb568161-3336-44ff-8c58-8568b8498d62&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fdocsend.com*2fview*2fb5asv6pv5cy38hfs&ValidityToken=L7ZNjvJ7YoZpUB5d9Iq3E8OQF5J07JumkmII93h4iIE*3d__;JSUlJSUl!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCaldEfzM$>  here

To talk through partners, timelines, and how you could participate as an investor, schedule here [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=903615cc-8f30-46ad-acb0-2ac61b9b07d1&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fcalendly.com*2fwalter-gomez-kc*2fpriority-investor-zoom-meeting-with-the-found-clone-1&ValidityToken=*2fLnWGFx7LNWONJhFzcs5QyxofWuaaJSbrHR2eeFfrJw*3d__;JSUlJSUlJQ!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCFekIeJU$> .

 

3) Investment Terms — priority pricing & limited blocks 

We are offering a priority tranche with two clear options for select, serious participants: 

*	Priority access price: $0.20 per share — available to qualified priority investors (minimums flexible; small allocations welcome). 

*	Limited block discount: $0.10 per share for $25,000+ commitments only — very limited blocks available; first-come, first-served. 

 

For context, our current implied institutional valuation is $26M (≈ $0.26 / share) — the priority pricing therefore represents meaningful upside for early participants prior to institutional pricing. Examples (illustrative): 

*	$5,000 at $0.20 → 25,000 shares → implied value @ $0.26 ≈ $6,500 

*	$10,000 at $0.20 → 50,000 shares → implied value @ $0.26 ≈ $13,000 

*	$25,000 at $0.10 → 250,000 shares → implied value @ $0.26 ≈ $65,000 (limited blocks) 

 

Full capital structure and assumptions are in the Semi-Annual Report [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=fb568161-3336-44ff-8c58-8568b8498d62&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fdocsend.com*2fview*2fb5asv6pv5cy38hfs&ValidityToken=L7ZNjvJ7YoZpUB5d9Iq3E8OQF5J07JumkmII93h4iIE*3d__;JSUlJSUl!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCaldEfzM$>  here.
 

To discuss allocation size and secure a block, please schedule here [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=903615cc-8f30-46ad-acb0-2ac61b9b07d1&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fcalendly.com*2fwalter-gomez-kc*2fpriority-investor-zoom-meeting-with-the-found-clone-1&ValidityToken=*2fLnWGFx7LNWONJhFzcs5QyxofWuaaJSbrHR2eeFfrJw*3d__;JSUlJSUlJQ!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCFekIeJU$> .

 

4) What we’ll provide if you’re interested 

If you’d like a tailored view, we will prepare a short, Personal Pro-Forma showing hypothetical allocations (e.g., $5k / $10k / $25k), projected implied values under conservative/base/upside cases, and any potential MVP access terms. This is for clarity only.  

 

Request your snapshot and schedule a call here [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=792d15ff-4f34-48f7-a1b2-4deacb75531c&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fcalendly.com*2fwalter-gomez-kc*2fpriority-investor-zoom-meeting-with-the-found-clone-1.&ValidityToken=hAVAyzkpG0Q5MA61eLk9RhETJstPpJm7rUnngmPEhCA*3d__;JSUlJSUl!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCiUhFmMo$> . 
 

See the underlying detail in the Semi-Annual Report here [crmri.crm.dynamics.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://crmri.crm.dynamics.com:443/api/EmailAnalytics/OpenLink?TrackingId=35b6c686-6216-f111-8341-002248359501&ConversationTrackingId=0cb5378b-35a1-452b-91c7-31c4a71ae6f9&ClientType=webclient&OrganizationId=5ebfdb8b-e858-f011-8ee5-002248282d3d&Salt=fb568161-3336-44ff-8c58-8568b8498d62&TenantId=384447b68ae44ec3a94a2c1b6d701332&RedirectUrl=https*3a*2f*2fdocsend.com*2fview*2fb5asv6pv5cy38hfs&ValidityToken=L7ZNjvJ7YoZpUB5d9Iq3E8OQF5J07JumkmII93h4iIE*3d__;JSUlJSUl!!AHh8_Z5O2w!k1BkdzcwV0S8gaGB48D28UPQBMl4oFMMUnFGEEZ3rd1hpKedSnD8fGALwWe0yAhKGBGZdP0AVUTJpToOBYFCaldEfzM$> .

Dear PN Partner,

Perceive Now [perceivenow.ai] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://perceivenow.ai/__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!ic-NU4FEKH9HvKIB8Yq-we9OIUqlVqqFezYyhXG6bMyBoHBXOype4Y3vozVPNp7G2Tn_PG7yqdfI_IsPzRfcKTOM$>  is headquartered in San Francisco, a city where the sourdough is sour, the hills are vertical, and the "Next Big Thing" is announced every 14 seconds.

But here is the controversial truth the Valley is trying to ignore: Generic AI is breaking in the real world. While everyone else is busy prompt-engineering their way to a better toaster, enterprise adoption is actually stalling because leadership is terrified of "black box" risks.

We decided to stop guessing and start measuring. We stress-tested our system against 1,000+ complex enterprise workflows. The results were a wake-up call for the giants:

*	The Failure Rate: Generic AI suffers from a 29% failure rate due to output corruption.

*	The David vs. Goliath Moment: In head-to-head tests, we beat OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic.

*	Zero-Error Runs: PN Kernel™ achieved 91.3% zero-error runs, compared to just 38.6% for GPT-4 and 18.3% for GPT-5.

*	The Consistency Gap: While GPT-4 struggles with a 51% consistency rate, we hit 96%.

*	The Reliability Flex: PN maintains 100% stage compliance—we don't skip steps, and we don't hallucinate data.


🚀 The 21-Day Thriller


We spent the last 18 months quietly proving our worth, generating $280k in revenue. Then, the market hit an inflection point. In just the last three weeks, the signal turned into a roar:

*	The Big Score: We signed a $1.85M yearly licensing MOI with Winsupply.

*	The Pipeline: We’ve secured a $10M+ pipeline across 30 companies.

*	Customer Validation: Our major verticals now center on PE Firms and Hospitals, moving into production with logos like Vodafone, P&G, Sanofi, Kantar, and Jefferson Health.

*	Real Impact: For Winsupply, we crush beyond timelines and save $20M per year.


🐱 Why This Is Personal


I didn’t start PN to follow a trend. I am a PhD in Electrical and Biomedical Engineering with 15+ years in corporate R&D. I’ve seen firsthand how brilliant technology dies in the "pilot stage" because it can't be audited. PN is my response to a decade of frustration.

Joining me is our CCO, Rachna Dayal. Her story is our ultimate validation: she was actually our customer first. She saw the power of PN, became an investor, and then decided she loved it so much she’d leave her role managing a $350M P&L at Johnson & Johnson to lead our commercial engine. When your customer becomes your investor, and then your C-suite partner, you know you’ve bridged the trust gap.

________________________________


🤝 The Ask


We successfully locked our $500k Pre-Seed round mid last year and used it to build the tech that is currently making the giants look like legacy software.

Now, the game has changed. Given the massive momentum of our $1.85M MOU with Winsupply, we only have $400k more for the seed round close.

We are ACTIVELY SAYING NO (I mean it!) to non-strategic investors. At this stage, we aren't looking for just cash; we are looking for partners who can open specific doors:

*	Connections to PE Operating partners.

*	Introductions to Hospital networks and healthcare enterprise P&L owners.

*	Access to Decision makers who are tired of AI pilots that never go live.

We are closing this bridge quickly to stay focused on execution. If you want to see the benchmarks that made the giants look small, or if you're interested in participating in the Seed round before the window slams shut, let’s talk.

Best,

Dr. Vinitha T U
Founder I CEO I Chief Scientist

Subject: Intro: TheOpenReality - Building 3D Data Engine for Embodied AI
Body:
Hi Andrew,

I hope this email finds you well. My name is Jerry Liu, co-founder of TheOpenReality. We’re building the synthetic data engine for embodied AI, enabling robotics companies to generate high-fidelity, physics-accurate 3D training scenes at scale.

We are a team combining deep technical research with strong commercialization expertise. Our founders include a PhD researcher and a Professor in Visual Computing and AI at the University of Cambridge, with prior experience at ETH Zurich, Google, and Disney, as well as business leadership from TikTok, EY-Parthenon, Harvard and MIT.

Why this matters now:
The race for embodied AI is bottlenecked by the scarcity of high-quality 3D training data. While others build general simulation platforms, we focus laser-sharp on solving this single problem: automating the generation of the 3D worlds at scale where robots learn. This is becoming a critical need for every major player from autonomous logistics to home assistant robotics.

We have validated feasibility with a working prototype, and are now raising our pre-seed round to build the founding team.

Given your deep expertise as an investor, our vision might be a good fit with your investment thesis. I’ve attached our brief pitch deck [docs.google.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Wz4dxrfl18HsL0B0WiH-sRA96gTb4_kazGk5tM26oMU/edit?usp=sharing__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!mdi0gwwVFn2JA7eDqG1vPeppBFwNNyMmKtelcyJHGip3yLuzCQzYED-A0g3n7Y2mHKkN8cAgrF0i70qruJsvXxs$>  for your review and would be grateful for the opportunity to discuss this further with you for 15-20 minutes next week.

Best regards,

Jerry Liu
Co-founder & CEO, TheOpenReality

Subject: Update | Beyond Capital Ventures Fund I
Body:
Hi, Andrew.



I’m following up on a note I sent earlier regarding Beyond Capital Ventures.



Since my last outreach, a meaningful amount of progress has occurred.



Over the past month, we’ve received commitments totaling between $250K and $350K, and initial wires are now coming in. As a result of that momentum, we’ve increased our current target close to a range of $500K to $1M by the end of this month.



To be clear, this message is not an offer to sell securities, nor is it a solicitation. I’m simply reconnecting to share an update and determine whether a conversation would be useful.

Beyond Capital Ventures is a pre-seed fund focused on AI application infrastructure, governance, and go-to-market platforms. We invest in founders building the systems that make AI usable inside real organizations.



If you are an accredited investor and would like to learn more about what we are building, I would be happy to schedule a brief LP-focused conversation.



You can request a 25-minute discussion here:
https://calendly.com/stevechaparro/bcv-25-min-call [calendly.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://calendly.com/stevechaparro/bcv-25-min-call__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!mIFYnIeDsV-jHNn563STi-Fq70F2uq7lJqOxy3dOa1AwTDC6KrP8mSeEBP3kR9tHrswrnezFUVwSP-Oz-tFkMYvs6w$> 


If now is not the right time, I appreciate you taking a moment to read this. I will continue to share periodic updates as the fund progresses.


All the best,


Steve Chaparro

Founder | Beyond Capital

Subject: Ex-Google/AWS: Capturing the 'Dark Data' of human dreams (€300k Pre-Seed)
Body:
Hi,
Did you know that 50% of dream memory is lost within 60 seconds of
waking? This "Dark Data" represents a massive, untapped frontier for
human creativity and emotional health.

I’m Dimitar, and I’m the founder of Remora. We’ve built the first
agentic interface that captures and visualizes dreams at the moment of
waking—before the memory vanishes.

Why Remora is different:

The 3-Second Wall: We’ve achieved a voice-to-visual latency of <3s
using a hybrid inference stack (on-device NPU + cloud hydration),
beating the biological clock of memory decay.

Privacy-by-Design: Our "Architect" tier runs in Trusted Execution
Environments (TEEs). We believe subconscious data is the most intimate
data on earth; it should never be training fodder for Big Tech.

Traction: Currently managing a waitlist of 100 early adopters with an
MVP that is already helping users track recurring archetypes and
emotional patterns.

With my background at Google and Amazon and an MBA in AI
Infrastructure, I’m focused on building a "Sovereign AI" company that
respects user intimacy while delivering 4K multimodal fidelity.

We are raising €300,000 to accelerate our on-device model distillation
and move out of private beta.

You can find our pitch deck and technical roadmap here:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://onepager.vc/remora__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!iqu1KgKYDjNlmv4qppwQAzxUmdDMX0XzXHsJYtI692Q78QxBbX254MhOQfVC_AmUNDS_x-4RXTPsXnczIw$

I’d love to show you how we’re turning the 1/3 of our lives spent
sleeping into a tool for waking growth. Are you free for a 10-minute
intro call next week?

Best regards,

Dimitar Founder&CEO, Remora

Startup Spotlight: DSALTA
Each week, we highlight one startup from the SeedScope ecosystem.

DSALTA - AI Compliance Automation for Modern SaaS

Valuation: $32.9M | Sector: Compliance & Security SaaS
Stage: Pre-Seed    | Location: Silicon Valley, CA, USA

DSALTA is building AI-powered compliance agents that automate audits, vendor risk monitoring, and security workflows across cloud, identity, and development environments. It addresses one of the biggest bottlenecks for growing startups: slow, manual, and costly compliance processes that delay enterprise deals and increase operational risk.

The company operates with TRL-9 technology, patented IP, and early paying customers, showing strong product validation and scalability. Positioned in the rapidly expanding GRC market and competing with players like Vanta, Drata, and SecureFrame, DSALTA is targeting a high-growth category with clear demand.

Fintech VC valuations race ahead in Europe 	
By Leah Hodgson, Deputy Editor, European Private Markets

Despite AI dominating the headlines with mega-rounds at astronomical valuations, fintech startups continue to command higher median VC valuations in Europe.

The median pre-money valuation for fintech startups reached €12.4 million (about $14.7 million) last year, according to PitchBook’s 2025 Annual European VC Valuations Report [url6380.news.pitchbook.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://url6380.news.pitchbook.com/uni/ls/click?upn=u001.d4n73BrX4Cuylw7SpSWIrf6GFa-2BaqM7ONnvsnswouzXFS0FW3n6-2BvWUcPzR2-2B3k-2FZpY46bFqxc11vS8IXzYByA-3D-3D_1V0_QXnTW6f9jV7ots26-2Fd0iCEYeiPzd5cXg8j8hhainmZFHt41V35rYgQg11oOqvj8g33gSCTSuCfqKjpEXvf-2Fp-2B8rZQNQmjY-2BDBwnBWB8V-2FAlhqVh7PZ-2B-2BaYHBDvI-2BhmldooW0iYxhAD4aK-2FYGqyQG-2BxIzlACh3bicFrmIVx2Q9G4ino5J8Z2JB-2FIakEk6zQzcdZmuuTfQJacWkx-2FxZFXV3VZa2LLK91jSALBKrJVENYGn56q0OBELvWchTHDz-2FSFD0puru-2B-2FMhGvMMFI9eiBBNNOkc1iAWlnOutoQzBPYfHAZLmSvRWaZ9VskaSu83jVkEedLlESdGci4t1CGN2Nww52jVyDNLBMQAKEg78JvAU-2BWmTJoI4ZqEmMbFASAYpWmy744QGkizfdoLD4yirqH7ImxR35njx9QK8oSPD7PvvHIo9q1rElfqKvmqVUmkN1KLTTuoJe9VOPR9Wl0rVogR4hTgr9-2BEpevSzoFvKf0fjsfLd0TpKILRW226HiwmOUnDSHob-2FolzXrkqZ6mdVuh0lyTNx9z4UrJC7ujVPTWi-2FU-3D__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!h8DQKRiV-QZBJjTmAyxduDSfglmHYhtrY3DyQB-w5rAUTavaHvXhEmZJwmI2F5WYJVhXpYN_xl_tznS4dnLj974EkH419JhoOm4$> . AI startups trail by 29%, while SaaS and life sciences are 26% and 21% below fintech, respectively.

The gap between fintech and AI is most pronounced at the earlier stages. The median valuation for pre-seed and seed fintech startups was €7.8 million—exactly €2 million more than for AI. In Series A-B rounds, fintech’s median valuation of €56.2 million far exceeded AI’s of €40.1 million.

The gap suggests that investors may be pricing in risk more aggressively for AI startups than for other sectors, such as fintech, even though terms sheets are signed more quickly and include fewer economic and governance protections.

Even at the early stages, fintech business models are well established, with clear comparables. Meanwhile, AI startups often build in an environment of rapidly changing technology, where long-term differentiation can be harder to assess early on.

Deal volume also plays a role in the valuation differences. Last year, there were three times as many VC deals for Europe’s AI startups as there were in fintech, broadening the mix of funded startups. As investors seek greater exposure to AI, these startups are raising capital more easily, whereas in fintech the bar remains higher for investment. Investors’ flight to quality in fintech pushed the median higher.

Still, this doesn’t mean that AI is losing momentum. Valuations for startups in the category are rising much faster year over year. The median overall for AI grew 21.6% from 2024, compared with 6% for fintech.

Whether AI valuations catch up with fintech will depend on whether the market corrects or funding continues to accelerate, as it has over the last few years. If funding for AI becomes tighter, investors could become more selective, focusing on startups with greater defensibility and, possibly, larger valuations.

By Rosie Bradbury, Senior Venture Capital Reporter

Nuclear fusion has captured Silicon Valley's imagination, even as investments in the technology break every venture capital norm.

A cohort of investors—a combination of ultra-wealthy individuals and deep-pocketed firms—enthusiastic about the promise of fusion energy is pouring more capital into its commercialization than ever. And that's despite the fact that it's still several scientific discoveries away from being a realistic option for the energy grid.

"There are some fusion bulls out there that have built up a position, some funds that have been in multiple fusion companies," said Leo Banchik, a partner at Voyager Ventures who leads its climate strategy.

Inertia, a fusion energy startup co-founded by Twilio founder Jeff Lawson, announced last week a $450 million Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners. In the last 12 months, 43 fusion energy startups have raised a total of $3.7 billion in venture capital, per PitchBook data—funding at a level unheard of for scientifically unproven technology.

"Nobody's demonstrated the [fusion] engine yet, so it's a bit like putting the cart before the horse," said Ryan McBride, a professor at the University of Michigan's nuclear engineering department.

By tapping into billionaires' checkbooks, fusion startups can access capital that will stay the course even if the technology isn't de-risked for years to come.

And that may well turn out to be the case. Some nuclear scientists have expressed doubts that Helion would be able to deliver on its promise of fusion power plants by 2028. Its competitor, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, has projected to bring electricity to the grid from its fusion plant in the early 2030s.

"I wish them well," said Mike Campbell, co-director of the University of California, San Diego's Fusion Engineering Institute. "It's an enormous challenge."

(Josie Doan/PitchBook News)	 
How much would you bet on a company that promises its AI can find your soulmate? Keeper, an AI matchmaking startup, is wagering $50,000 it can do it—and it's already collected on a few of these "marriage bounties."

Keeper and a slew of other startups are bringing AI to a long-running quest: using technology to find love. Match.com brought the relationship search online. Tawkify tried to make traditional matchmaking into a VC-scalable business. Tinder and Bumble made dating accessible with a swipe.

"Tinder digitized the bar," said Jake Kozloski, Keeper's founder and CEO. "We're digitizing something that's been around in many cultures for hundreds of years: matchmaking."

I'm Jacob Robbins, and this is the Weekend Pitch. You can reach me at jacob.robbins@pitchbook.com <mailto:jacob.robbins@pitchbook.com>  or on X @JacobERobbins [url6380.news.pitchbook.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://url6380.news.pitchbook.com/ls/click?upn=u001.d4n73BrX4Cuylw7SpSWIrf6GFa-2BaqM7ONnvsnswouzUeYojUVaFAf9AqgmCdlpwSF4HbNrWOFFxTNEFCHMBpwQ-3D-3DV876_QXnTW6f9jV7ots26-2Fd0iCEYeiPzd5cXg8j8hhainmZFHt41V35rYgQg11oOqvj8g33gSCTSuCfqKjpEXvf-2Fp-2B9uZzDHTG9Pdzhue0KW3Rgi7-2BP2aAe-2FlsSSeQu0d8gb-2BdyZaI9y-2BmuaKh7lRtTm-2B7D7Y-2Bcscfgyo9SKR4YCaX3LMzNTYldcmdU01k42DvAVjwFb-2BqajP83c-2FAGhCfxs0lG3DNeM1M8Gtz3kOU8U5bDmjFO0mhxrvInI7xpeUfqvwYUDsZWSQ2w9IiBMB7a5-2FCY60kxjJGIdTsp9lHdYPF2xh6FWTarJ-2FCLRZ50KEeeBzKjpBhQXfplTYovDIhJ8c4nuTzwLCuNNvi-2FLtCTvB7RvFZb1lGL8oAmdDkhLkHvZ8-2FJw2MFLmZWwkvjxPRhYjH7wBvhcMrdicJXvsHC9J2HyWJreGtnTBEXWm-2F8aYToNGIODPwCj3g4oh-2FKsXF60jI-2Fjz9zqC7-2F8B-2Fqr3zPLwBMNIPXXOpM710spk6ubQr7kw4wFQWmfnw9E6Uu3g2fahLa9ENHwV0Kw-2F8majcdz8jxNIZv-2B5GZxw2hMoWhQGPVQWDmg6VMYjUt29SQ-2Bu-2B9DjFA-3D-3D__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!luo6mrdQT07HBnQ7g6bObOAqiLLwMU7uzxosFuXksU2eETI1ENdtGqJWireQCn7tz6mS0Xpd9_RrhqTU6awEnSx4yjTvbWsbpvQ$> .

The terms of Keeper's marriage bounty are simple: you agree to pay $50,000 if its AI finds your "forever" person. You can deduct $5,000 from that bounty for each date chosen by its AI, giving Keeper an incentive to find your match quickly.

If it's kismet, which Keeper defines not just as getting married, but alternatively by living with each other for over a year, having children together or staying together for over 18 months, you pay the remainder of the bounty.

VCs are taking an interest in this new cohort. Keeper raised a $4 million pre-seed round in December. Ditto, an AI matchmaking startup for college students, raised $9.2 million in February. And Known, a startup using voice AI to match singles, raised $9.7 million, also in December.

But the business of online dating is tough.

We’re excited to invite you to PearX’s W26 Virtual Demo Day. 




When: 10am PT on Tuesday, April 7th

Where: https://demoday.pear.vc [us.list-manage.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://us.list-manage.com/-hIppG8xyW7?e=fe8ffcc998&c2id=d0b3bd8bfcc4080c6e7b5c473a37bebf__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!mSXtivAQUmrqf51mEsqSkb-CQoIxTNEBIKDayYqsxzYYx8EHhEFk8gMuiEI3gMIwZMfVqsTnHtyklVnJ4w$>

About Demo Day


This is the first opportunity to connect with these teams, before they are announced to the world. 




The event will be live streamed on the demo day website linked above. Through the site, you can also express interest in companies you’d like to invest in, and directly connect with the founders. 







If you have any colleagues or fellow investors that may be interested in PearX demo day, please feel free to forward this invite to them. 




Please RSVP [us.list-manage.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://us.list-manage.com/VD364Ww2DeR?e=fe8ffcc998&c2id=d0b3bd8bfcc4080c6e7b5c473a37bebf__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!mSXtivAQUmrqf51mEsqSkb-CQoIxTNEBIKDayYqsxzYYx8EHhEFk8gMuiEI3gMIwZMfVqsTnHtyAVm8Y2g$>  if you’re able to join us. 





About PearX


PearX is Pear VC’s exclusive small-batch accelerator for pre-seed companies. PearX has consistently backed remarkable founders that have collectively raised over $2 billion in funding, including the founders of companies such as Cardless, Federato, Affinity, Glide, Bobyard, and many more. 




Best, 

Pear VC Team

Subject: Introduction
Body:
Hi Andrew,

My name is Ali Azim, and I'm the Investor Relations Manager of Zonilyze.

Zonilyze turns zoning and land use rules into plain-English answers with citations to the underlying code, so real estate professionals can screen deals faster, and defend the result.

A few quick bullets:

*	Cambridge, MA alpha is live today (code-cited outputs, not a black box)
*	We're scaling the same playbook across additional high-volume markets
*	Raising up to $600k pre-seed on a $5M post-money SAFE to finish the partner-grade MVP and launch a small set of paid pilots in early 2026

If this is in your lane, open to sharing a short deck or setting up a brief 15-minute intro?

Thanks,

Ali

 

 

-- 

ALI AZIM

INVESTOR RELATIONS MANAGER

Elon Musk's argument for merging SpaceX and xAI into the biggest VC-backed company Silicon Valley has ever seen is that building data centers in space will be necessary for the coming AI revolution.

"When we made this investment in 2023, we were laughed off Twitter as this being too audacious," said Scott Hartley, co-founder and GP at pre-seed firm Everywhere Ventures, speaking of its investment in Starcloud. The startup, backed by Nvidia, is on a mission to construct data centers in space.

But turning science fiction into reality isn't easy. A constellation of satellites on the scale SpaceX is imagining would represent a 100-fold increase in satellites in Earth's orbit, according to leading astrophysicist John McDowell, an expert in orbital systems.

Still, investors are not deterred, seeing orbital data centers as an answer to the challenge of providing the infrastructure the AI revolution needs.

VC space-tech investing has rebounded over the past 12 months, according to PitchBook data. From 2024 to 2025, deal value increased 65% from $6.7 billion to $11.1 billion.

"Space exploration is incredibly capital intensive," McDowell said. "It's very much an open question whether this is the right move."

PE mid-market is struggling to rediscover its animal spirits 	
By Madeline Shi, Sr. Private Equity Reporter

Despite high-profile mega-deals keeping the mood up, sentiment at the smaller end of the private equity market remains muted. Concerningly, it's not clear how or when this might change.

The pace of capital deployment and exits in the middle market has yet to catch up with the upper end, while anecdotes from dealmakers, banks and advisers to PE firms point to a lack of animal spirits across the market and to sharply contrasting fates of pristine assets and the rest.

Companies considered high-quality are still trading at premium multiples, while there was agreement that those with "some hair on them" barely attract any interest or draw bids that come in at a significant discount to sellers' expectations.

High customer concentration, the perceived risk of management turnover and operating in a sector whose fortunes are closely linked to the wider economic cycle are enough to derail deals in today's middle market.

"Buyer mentality is still quite disciplined, so you end up seeing a tale of two cities," said Jared Stein, the co-founder of Monogram Capital Partners.

As a buyer in many of these transactions, Stein said he often sees a disconnect between asset quality and the price demanded by sellers.

Concerns around the management team are a common source of friction, said one partner with an international law firm, citing a recent attempted sale of a company in the business services sector.

While the company's financial performance tracked roughly in line with projections, its chief executive told potential buyers he might not remain in the role. Buyer interest waned, and the transaction almost stalled.

A similar sentiment permeated the automotive collision repair industry, which had a challenging year in 2025. Assets in the sector that were not considered premium saw virtually no buyer interest, an M&A dealmaker told PitchBook. This marks a contrast to previous cycles, when there was always one or two bidders.

"In 2021 … it was almost like the dot-com boom era where you had all these assets that needed some work but continued to be powered by the sheer exuberance of the market," said A&O Shearman's Alain Dermarkar. This is no longer the case, he added.

Transaction News Summary


Devon Energy and Coterra Energy agreed [cdp5.tracking.e360.salesforce.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://cdp5.tracking.e360.salesforce.com/click?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..CgSBwsV2M_UCOl5ktxdV1GDxtppidiRRtMXUvxdY11Y__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!lb-jsvJNR-rDIhYbl9of3R9tRBDlKXxERj6_PQxBcWLlHBz9guxYXzCxFpfl7NvzpBBU-lHVyTNKCf76-_n1Fab8tZiH$>  to a $58B all-stock merger, forming a scaled Delaware Basin operator with more than a decade of inventory and $1B of projected annual pre-tax synergies. The combined E&P company will uphold its shareholder return framework with a 31.5-cent quarterly dividend and a $5B repurchase authorization.

Consolidation momentum on the day extended beyond traditional energy deals into frontier technology, with Elon Musk reportedly in advanced talks [cdp5.tracking.e360.salesforce.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://cdp5.tracking.e360.salesforce.com/click?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..zXAj61-exuP9oRo7BEmWR0xOmZ2Xaw3I0JRTqUrRVSE__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!lb-jsvJNR-rDIhYbl9of3R9tRBDlKXxERj6_PQxBcWLlHBz9guxYXzCxFpfl7NvzpBBU-lHVyTNKCf76-_n1FfL94VZI$>  to merge SpaceX and xAI. Such a deal would unite an $800B aerospace platform with a $200B AI business as Musk explores space-based data center infrastructure to meet surging compute demand.

Sponsor-backed M&A remained active as well. ESAB agreed to acquire [cdp5.tracking.e360.salesforce.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://cdp5.tracking.e360.salesforce.com/click?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..951ZheoYpAqMLo0jilWYQN2-EXClaWZiig7PG-3ag6w__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!lb-jsvJNR-rDIhYbl9of3R9tRBDlKXxERj6_PQxBcWLlHBz9guxYXzCxFpfl7NvzpBBU-lHVyTNKCf76-_n1FfVO6LZ0$>  Eddyfi Technologies for $1.45B, delivering an exit for Previan and CDPQ through a deal funded with cash, debt, and $318M of committed equity. Transaction News Summary


Francisco Partners led the day’s sponsor-backed M&A activity, closing its roughly $2.2B acquisition of Apple device management specialist Jamf and removing the platform from the public markets.

Sponsor-backed M&A stayed active across sectors. Halozyme Therapeutics acquired preclinical platform Surf Bio in a deal worth up to $400M, including $300M upfront plus milestones, expanding its drug-delivery technology. Blackstone-backed Rover Group added cat-sitting marketplace Meowtel as an independent-brand consumer add-on.

In financials, Permira agreed to acquire a significant minority stake in fund administrator Carne Group at a €1.4B valuation, enabling Vitruvian’s exit while founder John Donohoe retains majority control into an expected Q3 close.

Strategic deal chatter included SpaceX weighing a potential merger with Tesla or xAI alongside IPO planning that could target a $50B raise at a $1.5T valuation. In sports and media, BMG is considering a bid for Concord at up to $7B, while KKR is negotiating to lead a €2.75B minority investment into CVC’s Global Sport Group vehicle.

Fundraising headlines were led by Amazon’s talks to invest up to $50B in OpenAI as part of a round that could reach $100B and value the company at $830B, with NVIDIA and SoftBank also potential participants. Redwood Materials closed an expanded $425M Series E with Capricorn and Goldman Sachs Alternatives continuing support and Google joining as a new investor. Finally, Poetiq emerged from stealth with a $45.8M seed round co-led by FYRFLY Venture Partners and Surface Ventures.

Read on for a rundown of all the latest transaction news.

Carlyle anchored a heavy sponsor-backed M&A tape with its agreement to buy the majority of Lukoil’s international business outside Kazakhstan, a deal structured for full OFAC compliance as a February 28 deadline approaches. While financial terms were not disclosed, the asset base spans European refineries and over 5,000 fuel stations.

Strategic buyer activity followed close behind. VSE Corp. agreed to acquire GenNx360-backed Precision Aviation Group for about $2.03B, including $1.75B of cash, $275M of equity and a potential $125M earnout. In sponsor-style capital formation, Sony Music partnered with GIC to establish a joint venture dedicated to acquiring music copyrights, with Sony Bank also participating and $2B to $3B of planned deployment. Prosperity Bancshares rounded out the headline M&A slate with its $2B acquisition of Stellar Bancorp, a Texas consolidation move that will integrate Stellar’s 52 locations and $10.8B of assets into Prosperity’s footprint.

The deal pipeline stayed crowded in Europe and Asia. CVC is in advanced talks for DSM-Firmenich’s animal nutrition unit at €2.2B, while Bain, Blackstone, EQT and TPG advanced in bidding for UK supplements group Vitabiotics at around £900M. Blackstone is also reportedly negotiating to become majority shareholder of Hong Kong’s New World Development, a rare potential shift away from legacy family control.

Growth capital continued flowing to AI and healthcare platforms. Decagon raised a $250M Series D led by Coatue and Index at a $4.5B valuation to scale conversational AI agents, while Tenpoint Therapeutics secured $235M to support its presbyopia launch. Digital asset infrastructure provider Talos added $45M in a Series B extension, bringing total Series B funding to $150M at a $1.5B valuation, and Munich-based robotics player RobCo raised $100M in a Series C to expand physical AI deployments across the US and Europe.

Subject: Introducing AI powered workspace for researchers
Body:
Hi –

I’m the founder of Roadrunner AI. We’ve been working on an AI powered workspace for researchers to address time wasted on fragmented research tools used for scientific research in academia and industry.  After a period of building and testing, we’re now beginning early discussions around a small pre-seed round to support go-to-market execution and product engineering.

Given your growing portfolio and platforms I thought it could be worth connecting. Would it make sense to share a brief overview so you can see if this is relevant on your side?


Best,
John

(Courtesy of Waabi)	 
By Rosie Bradbury, Senior Venture Capital Reporter

Autonomous-trucking company Waabi raised a $750 million Series C led by Khosla Ventures and G2 Venture Partners. Uber committed an additional $250 million in strategic financing, bringing the total new capital to $1 billion.

This is the largest VC round raised by a startup based in Canada. And it marks the biggest indication yet that Uber is jumping back into the self-driving car race, as the deal includes a partnership to roll out Waabi-powered robotaxis.

Waabi's founder and CEO, Raquel Urtasun, was an executive at Uber's self-driving program from 2017 to 2021, during the height of the company's autonomous driving efforts. After settling a trade secret theft lawsuit from Waymo in 2018, Uber slowly wound down much of its investment in the arena, including selling its autonomous driving unit to Aurora in late 2020.

Waymo's rapid fleet expansion across the US has helped fuel renewed investor interest in self-driving tech. Waymo, owned by Alphabet, is reportedly in talks to raise $15 billion in new funding at a valuation of over $100 billion.

Because developing self-driving vehicles requires a significant upfront capital investment, few VC-backed companies have reached viability and deployed fleets. Alphabet classifies its Waymo business under "other bets," a unit essentially including everything not related to Google services, which altogether reported a loss of $1.4 billion in its latest earnings report.

So rivaling Alphabet's deep pockets won't be easy. "It's still important to pay attention to cost-per-mile," said Jonathan Geurkink, senior emerging tech analyst at PitchBook. "All those sensors, processing and human oversight costs a lot. If Uber scaled this up, it would hit their P&L pretty hard as a public company." Founded in 2009, Uber only reported its first profitable year in 2023.

Uber has been making some measured moves to add autonomous driving back into its business. It first invested in Waabi in 2021, and in 2024 it announced a partnership to offer Waymo rides to some customers in Austin and Atlanta.

Early-career allocators push for pay transparency 	
By Jessica Hamlin, Senior Funds Columnist

When Owen Lu was an analyst at Vanderbilt University's endowment, he started thinking about his next move. He quickly ran into a wall.

"I remember going on Wall Street Oasis [forum] and asking, 'Hey guys, what are exit opportunities for LP roles?' and 'What do LPs usually do?' And all the bankers would reply, 'I don't know, good luck,'" Lu said.

For early-career LPs like Owen, who at the time had less than five years of investment experience under his belt, salary expectations and career opportunities are less apparent—and less standardized—than in other pockets of finance.

"Everyone sort of knows what a lawyer in big law makes in New York, and everyone sort of knows what a PE associate makes two years out of banking. But with the LP space, it's really a black box," Lu said.

Industry compensation surveys from executive search firms like Korn Ferry and Heidrick & Struggles offer insights into carry and base salaries at PE firms and investment banks, but data from investment offices at universities, family offices and private foundations are more difficult to compile.

Lu and his co-founders at the Emerging Allocators Association, an industry group of early to mid-career allocators, have attempted to fill this information gap. In January, EAA released its first compensation survey.

According to the survey, which received around 140 responses, base salaries for allocators with less than eight years of experience tend to hover in the $100,000 to $150,000 band, with average bonuses increasing incrementally per year of experience.

For example, 72% of respondents with three years of experience reported receiving a base salary of $100,000 to $150,000, and 48% of this group reported an annual cash bonus of less than $25,000.

Over half of respondents with exactly seven years of experience reported a base salary in that same band, but 61% said their annual bonus surpassed $50,000.

"We wanted to drive a little bit more transparency on the back end for people to have more negotiating power going into [job interview] processes," said Grace Brown, an investment manager at Vanderbilt's endowment and co-founder of the EAA.

Dakota tracked more than 1,800 pension investments totaling over $192B in 2025, with activity concentrated in private equity, private credit, and real assets. The report highlights how large checks still drive a meaningful share of dollars, how improved market conditions supported pacing, why private credit is gaining share, and what the setup looks like for 2026.

Transaction News Summary


Big-ticket dealmaking was relatively sparse, though several notable fundraising rounds suggested investors remain willing to write meaningful checks for high-conviction stories.

China-based Anta Sports’s €1.5B purchase of a 29% stake in Puma from the Pinault family’s Artémis marks a notable strategic cross-border play in global sportswear this year. The deal deepens Anta’s influence in premium Western brands, longside Salomon, Arc’teryx and Wilson, while giving Artémis balance-sheet flexibility after its CAA acquisition.

Beyond marquee names, sponsor activity remained active across services and healthcare. Pollen Street’s buyout of fund-services platform Tutman points to sustained demand for governance and administration infrastructure, while Adenia’s investment in Egypt’s Parkville Pharmaceuticals reflects appetite for regional healthcare champions. Blackstone-backed Sia also pushed further into the US with its acquisition of Kaiser Associates, sharpening its strategy and PE advisory capabilities.

Funding markets, meanwhile, stayed firmly open for scaled platforms. Notion’s $270M employee tender at an $11B valuation, backed by GIC, Sequoia and Index, signaled continued confidence in productivity software, while Oviva’s €200M raise and Mubadala-led investment into Property Finder highlighted strong sovereign and growth equity support for digital health and consumer internet assets. Crypto infrastructure also stayed in favor, as Mesh crossed unicorn territory following a $75M Series C round.

Read on for a rundown of all the latest transaction news.

Transaction News Summary


The week began with several big-ticket deals, headlined by Zijin Gold’s $4B-plus acquisition of Allied Gold and Leidos’ $2.4B purchase of ENTRUST Solutions from Kohlberg.

Zijin Gold’s takeover of Allied Gold represents the Hong Kong-based company’s aggressive expansion into Africa, adding assets across Côte d’Ivoire, Mali and Ethiopia. Strategic buyers were equally active stateside, as Leidos agreed to acquire ENTRUST Solutions to deepen its engineering and infrastructure services footprint, while IonQ moved to vertically integrate its quantum ambitions with a $1.8B deal for semiconductor foundry SkyWater Technology.

In financial services, CVC pushed further into global credit with its $1.2B acquisition of Marathon Asset Management, fusing European scale with US opportunistic credit expertise, while on the consumer side, Daniel Kretinsky’s EP Group advanced plans to consolidate control of French retailer Fnac Darty in a €1.1B tender offer.

The pipeline remains well stocked, with several heavyweight assets lining up for potential exits. Volkswagen’s diesel engine unit Everllence could fetch up to €6B as Blackstone, Brookfield and others circle, while Exxon Mobil is marketing more than $1B of Eagle Ford shale assets. UK healthcare and European digital infrastructure are also in play, with Spire Healthcare and Retelit both exploring strategic options amid renewed sponsor interest.

Fundraising, meanwhile, stayed firmly open for scaled innovation. Frontier AI drew eye-catching checks, including Ricursive Intelligence’s $300M Series A at a $4B valuation and Synthesia’s $200M raise valuing the company at $4B, while life sciences investors backed Corxel Pharmaceuticals with nearly $300M to advance next-generation cardiometabolic therapies.

Subject: ZapHealth | Pre-Seed Fundraising Opening
Body:
Early look at ZapHealth’s pre-seed round — employer-paid wellness + telehealth with measurable business signals. 
Pre-seed now open 	 [lucirastrategystudio.us6.list-manage.com] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://lucirastrategystudio.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b1d67d7eb1ce67f199f0d22ec&id=22b15adec5&e=a347beeef7__;!!AHh8_Z5O2w!llALZyfJcYUAXcA_G9yKQUuKTNPHsVfUTO8f0a0uxsCO_yhaBt1rd8OBcKwZgOzPDjG715qT2EFNV9_ImEfPjHv5$>  	
	
Hi, 

 

I’m reaching out from Lucira, our venture strategy studio, to share an early look at one of our newest incubated ventures: ZapHealth. We’ve officially opened ZapHealth’s pre-seed round. 

 

ZapHealth is an employer-paid occupational + preventative wellness platform built to solve a common gap: companies spend heavily on benefits and wellness, but lack visibility into real usage and how those offerings affect productivity, retention, and healthcare costs. 

 

ZapHealth brings services together (including unlimited telehealth and everyday physical, mental, and occupational support) and pairs them with an employer-side system that translates engagement into clear, measurable business signals—so wellness becomes operational and ROI-driven through one integrated platform.

China takes the lead in new drug development—with no plans to let it go 	
By Ben Zercher, Sr. Research Analyst, Biotech and Pharma

The stakes are rising for how quickly US capital and clinical infrastructure can keep pace with China's rapid advancements in biopharma innovation.

For decades, the US led the way in novel therapeutic development, backed by deep capital markets and the world's most mature clinical trial ecosystem. That paradigm is changing rapidly as China's investments in new technologies and clinical modernization translate into attractive therapies ready to be tested in patients.

That shift was on display in 2025, when licensing to Western pharmaceutical firms surged and large- and mid-cap biopharma companies raced to secure Chinese-origin programs. Many of these deals centered on early clinical-stage candidates across new therapeutic approaches, reinforcing China's growing role as a source for innovation.

The backdrop is the recently enacted US Biosecure Act, which aims to reduce reliance on Chinese-linked biopharma development. Although supply chain security is a defensible priority, the policy could introduce cost and execution pressure for early-stage companies trying to scale new programs into the clinic.

VC funding trends are aligned: Legacy pipelines are being meaningfully displaced as investors focus on emerging technologies. Combined with the early signs of a reopening IPO window, China's biopharma ecosystem looks capable of funding and scaling innovation domestically, intensifying competition with US biopharma.
